name: Salmon Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. PREHEAT OVEN TO 325°F.
2. Remove and discard the crust from the bread. Tear the bread into pieces and set aside.
3. Melt the butter in a skillet over medium heat on the stove.
4. Add the onions and cook, stirring for 5 minutes or until softened.
5. Add the wine, bring to a boil and cook 1 minute.
6. Add the bread and cook, stirring for 5 minutes.
7. Scrape the mixture into a mixing bowl.
8. Roughly cut the whitefish and salmon into 1-inch pieces and add to the bread in the mixing bowl.
9. Add the salt, pepper, coriander, and nutmeg and mix.
10. Place the mixture in a food processor and process until smooth.
OR
Fit a meat grinder with a medium die and grind the fish mixture twice.
11. Transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl and place the bowl over a pan of ice water.
12. Add the eggs and egg whites and mix well.
13. Slowly add the cream.
14. Pack the mixture into a 9-by-5-by-3-inch glass, ceramic, or metal loaf pan.
15. Tap the pan on a counter to firmly pack it.
16. Cover tightly with a double layer of aluminum foil.
17. Place the loaf pan in a larger pan and fill with boiling water until it rises halfway to the top of the loaf pan.
18. Place in the oven for 1 hour.
19. Remove the pate from the oven and its water bath.
20. Place the loaf pan on a baking sheet.
21. Cool for 1 hour.
22. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
23. To remove, run a knife around the edges of the pate.
24. Dip the loaf pan in hot water for a minute.
25. Turn out onto a plate.
26. Serve with toast or crackers.
